title: Geophila tenuis
intro: Geophila tenuis, also known as Spreading Lousewort, is a low-growing, herbaceous plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its delicate, white or pinkish flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Geophila tenuis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Geophila tenuis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.
